channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Four New Zealand Lawmakers Banned From China After Taiwan Trip
item.description: New Zealand’s foreign minister was “surprised” to hear about the one-year travel ban, a spokesperson said, given lawmakers have visited Taiwan for years without issues.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:credit: Presidential Office of Taiwan, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Vice President Bi-khim Hsiao of Taiwan, in red, posing for a group photo with, from left to right, the New Zealand lawmakers Laura McClure, Maureen Pugh, Duncan Webb and David Wilson at the presidential office in Taipei, Taiwan, in May.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Agree to Renew Cease-Fire
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed on Wednesday to renew a cease-fire after U.S.-mediated talks in Washington. The agreement depends on the cooperation of Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militia.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:description: Daniel Holler, the State Department chief of staff, speaking during a meeting between Israeli and Lebanese delegations in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: La ambición de Elon Musk por el dinero extraterrestre
item.description: El empresario pone la mira en la muy rentable órbita terrestre baja.
item.dc:creator: Clive Irving
item.category: United States Agency for International Development

item.title: South Korea’s Governing Democratic Party Sweeps Local Elections but Faces Setback in Seoul Mayor’s Race
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung’s Democratic Party had hoped to consolidate its influence and extend his political momentum.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:description: Democratic Party members and lawmakers, gathered at the National Assembly in Seoul on Wednesday, cheered after exit polls predicted a decisive win for the party in local elections.

item.title: World Cup Commute by the Numbers: Not Many Options, Few of Them Cheap
item.description: When the World Cup arrives in New Jersey, fans will face some difficult choices: Uber surge pricing, $225 parking spots and few seats on the bus.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:description: Options for travel from New York City to World Cup matches at MetLife Stadium will be limited.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 4, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Reacts to Another Reality Star Wooing Voters
item.description: Michael Kosta wasn’t impressed to see that the L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt “got a second-season pickup” after Tuesday’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘beneficent’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 12 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: beneficent
item.description: This word has appeared in 12 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: With His New Museum, Obama Offers a Trip to a Parallel America
item.description: The presidential center that opens this month seems out of step with the zeitgeist of the moment and produces emotional reactions among the discontented of the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker and Jamie Kelter Davis

item.title: ¿Por qué este año a nadie le importa el Mundial?
item.description: No es solo porque se celebre en Estados Unidos bajo el mandato de Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: David Wallace-Wells

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz Is Blocked. The World Is Adjusting.
item.description: The longer we go without oil from the Persian Gulf, the less we’ll need it.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Smart
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Aleksey Kondratyev for The New York Times

item.title: Europa ya no quiere depender tecnológicamente de EE. UU.
item.description: La Unión Europea adelanta cómo planea tomar las riendas de sus centros de datos, semiconductores y computación en la nube.
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano

media:content.media:description: European Union flags flutter outside the European Commission headquarters in Brussels.

item.title: En plena guerra, el presidente del fútbol iraní trabaja para que su selección vaya al Mundial
item.description: Mehdi Taj dijo que su federación ha estado tratando con la FIFA, y no con Estados Unidos, para intentar obtener visados para que Irán juegue en el torneo que comienza la próxima semana.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mehdi Taj being interviewed by a reporter in Tehran last month. Mr. Taj said mixed messages from Washington had created confusion over Iran’s participation in the World Cup.

item.title: Amid War, Iran’s Soccer Leader Works to Get His Team to the World Cup
item.description: Mehdi Taj said his federation has been dealing with FIFA, not the United States, in trying to obtain visas for Iran to play in the tournament that begins next week.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mehdi Taj being interviewed by a reporter in Tehran last month. Mr. Taj said mixed messages from Washington had created confusion over Iran’s participation in the World Cup.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Warrior King Reconquers India as a Hindu Hero
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, June 4, 2024.

item.title: Corrections: June 4, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, June 4, 2026.

item.title: Sherrill Says Immigration Officials Won’t Let Her Visit Detention Center
item.description: The governor said the Department of Homeland Security’s actions at Delaney Hall raise “serious questions.”
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill of New Jersey and Senator Andy Kim, right, have expressed concerns about conditions at the Delaney Hall immigration detention center.

item.title: We Interrupt This Knicks Victory for a Political Ad Starring Mamdani
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani lent his political star power to an unusual TV ad promoting three Democrats running for House seats in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

item.title: Pace Gallery Cuts 50 Artists and 50 Staff Amid Art Market Challenges
item.description: A challenging art market is forcing even an established player to contract.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin

media:content.media:credit: Clement Pascal for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “The whole art gallery art system became too big, too commercial, too impersonal and too corporate,” Marc Glimcher, the chief executive of Pace Gallery, said.

item.title: How Does LA Count Election Ballots? It Begins With a Dog Sniff.
item.description: Counting ballots in California is a complex and lengthy undertaking. In Los Angeles, the choreographed process includes canines, humans and machines.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department dog checked incoming ballots for harmful substances at the Ballot Processing Center in the City of Industry, Calif., in November.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Renew Cease-Fire, Calling for Evacuation of Hezbollah Operatives
item.description: The agreement depends on the cooperation of Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militia. The cease-fire could remove an obstacle in the talks between the United States and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Daniel Holler, the State Department chief of staff, speaking during a meeting between Israeli and Lebanese delegations in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Flesh-Eating Pest Confirmed in U.S. Cattle
item.description: The New World screwworm was found in a calf in South Texas, the Agriculture Department said, the first case in the country since the 1960s.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Draper

media:content.media:description: The New World screwworm fly has been moving north since it was detected at the Guatemalan-Mexican border in 2024.

item.title: Why Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass Has to Fight for Her Job
item.description: Ms. Bass, a Democrat, has had a roller-coaster first term. She now faces the first runoff since 2005 for an incumbent mayor in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan

media:content.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass, center, campaigning at a community center in Plaza de Valle in Los Angeles, last month.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 4, 2026
item.description: Joe Deeney takes us on a solving spree.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Francesca Forquet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ready, set, solve.

item.title: Israel’s High Court Rejects Ban on Red Cross Visits for Palestinian Prisoners
item.description: Israel forbade the visits, which allow the Red Cross to monitor prisoners’ conditions, in 2023. Justices said the policy violated Israeli and international law.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
International Committee of the Red Cross

media:content.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the Red Cross in Gaza last year.

item.title: Voters in California City Favor Permanent Ban on Data Centers
item.description: Monterey Park, outside Los Angeles, appeared to become the first U.S. city where voters passed such a measure.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz

media:content.media:credit: Robert Gauthier/Los Angeles Times, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A mostly vacant business park in Monterey Park, Calif., where plans to build a data center galvanized the community in opposition.

item.title: La Cámara de Representantes de EE. UU. vota por el fin de la guerra de Irán
item.description: La aprobación de la resolución legislativa fue una notable reprimenda a Trump y a su gestión de la guerra, después de que el mandatario haya rechazado cualquier esfuerzo por frenar su poder.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro

media:content.media:description: La votación del miércoles fue el último reflejo de las divisiones entre los republicanos del Congreso y el presidente Trump en una serie de asuntos, a medida que sus intereses divergen en el periodo previo a las elecciones intermedias al Congreso.

item.title: Massie, 3 Other House Republicans Broke From Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
item.description: Four Republicans from different ideological factions crossed party lines to vote with Democrats in favor of reining in the president’s power to wage war unilaterally.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: 8 Indicted in $4.5 Million Scheme Involving Cheese, Meat and Cigarettes
item.description: Prosecutors say a group collaborated with a hacking syndicate that used phishing schemes to steal shipment information.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

item.title: Trump Says Iran War Is ‘Not a Big Thing’ for U.S.
item.description: It was the latest attempt by President Trump to flip the narrative on a conflict that he once said would lead to quick and decisive victory.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the Oval Office on Wednesday.

item.title: Louisiana ICE Facility Mistreated Immigrants, Federal Investigators Say
item.description: A report by the Department of Homeland Security’s internal watchdog described officers putting one man in a chokehold and stabbing another with a pen.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Nicholas Nehamas

media:content.media:description: The Winn Correctional Center, an ICE detention facility in Winnfield, La. Multiple instances of mistreatment were documented at the correctional center last year.

item.title: Republicans Begin to Test the Limits of Trump’s Power by Flexing Their Own
item.description: The president’s unilateral and retributive style of governing is starting to hit a wall in both chambers of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump departing the White House in May. On Wednesday, four House Republicans sided with Democrats to demand Mr. Trump withdraw U.S. forces from the conflict with Iran.

item.title: Ted Danson Apologizes for Blackface Roast of Whoopi Goldberg in 1993
item.description: The actor’s monologue drew condemnation from figures including David N. Dinkins, New York’s first Black mayor.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: Whoopi Goldberg and Ted Danson before the Friars Club roast in 1993.

item.title: Conductora sin mano derecha es detenida por usar el celular con la mano derecha
item.description: Su caso, por supuesto, fue desestimado.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

item.title: Obscure Group With Trump Ties Plans to Route Funds to His Allies for Legal Fights
item.description: A nonprofit called the Lawfare Defense Fund has raised $36 million to support those who consider themselves unjust victims of the legal system with grants to pay for litigation.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Ken Bensinger

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump recently retreated from a highly divisive plan for a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people who claim they were inappropriately targeted for prosecution, potentially including those who participated in the Jan. 6, 2021, riot.

item.title: Arizona, Nevada Agree to Trade for Desalinated Pacific Ocean Water
item.description: A decade-old treatment plant in San Diego County, Calif., could leave more water in the Colorado River for states facing severe shortages.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance

media:content.media:credit: Sandy Huffaker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: From left, Dan Denham of the San Diego County Water Authority; Scott Cameron of the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation; Tom Buschatzke of the Arizona Department of Water Resources; and Colby Pellegrino of the Southern Nevada Water Authority toasted the agreement at the Carlsbad Desalination Plant in California on Wednesday.

item.title: NASA Says Goodbye to its Longtime Mars MAVEN Mission
item.description: The space agency announced that the MAVEN spacecraft, which has circled Mars for more than a decade, is being decommissioned.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

media:content.media:description: An artist’s concept of the Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution, or MAVEN, which has been orbiting around Mars since 2014.

item.title: In Alabama Ruling, Supreme Court Signals Limited Role for Federal Courts in Redistricting Fights
item.description: In an emergency ruling on Tuesday night, the court’s conservative majority gave a first glimpse into congressional district battles under a weakened Voting Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle

media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court’s emergency Alabama ruling provided a look into how it may handle congressional district battles going forward.

item.title: The House Votes to End the Iran War
item.description: Also, the N.B.A. finals tips off. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: SpaceX IPO to Be Largest Ever at $135 Share Price
item.description: The $135 share price means Elon Musk’s rocket maker is poised to exceed the 2019 initial public offering of Saudi Aramco in both valuation and money raised.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac

media:content.media:description: SpaceX’s Starship rocket lifted off last month during a test flight from Starbase in Texas.

item.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Removing Job Protections From Federal Workers
item.description: The order is a response to resistance during the first Trump administration, when senior career government officials routinely pushed back against policies that appeared to exceed legal boundaries.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Sullivan

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the Oval Office on Wednesday.

item.title: How Each House Member Voted on the Iran War Powers Resolution
item.description: The House passed a measure to rein in the use of military force in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Wu and Martín González Gómez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: ‘I Love It’: Trump Is Still in Favor of $1.8 Billion Payout Fund
item.description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, said on Tuesday that the administration was “not moving forward with the fund, period,” after the plan drew bipartisan backlash.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:description: President Trump on Wednesday in the Oval Office. In one of his first official acts of his second term, Mr. Trump issued a grant of clemency to the hundreds charged in connection with the Capitol attack.

item.title: Trump Agitates Brazil’s Politics, Again
item.description: President Trump appears ready to lend a hand to a new right-wing ally ahead of a crucial election this year that will be the biggest test for Latin America’s left.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova

media:content.media:description: Flávio Bolsonaro in Brasília following his meeting with President Trump at the White House.

item.title: House Advances New Sanctions on Russia and Aid to Ukraine
item.description: After G.O.P. leaders blocked additional aid to Ukraine, six Republicans and an independent joined Democrats to force the measure to the floor against the wishes of the speaker.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison

media:content.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Damage from Russian strikes in Ukraine’s Donetsk region in February. The House took up legislation that aims to target Russia’s oil and gas sector, its primary source of wartime revenue.

item.title: Africans Exploring Africa
item.description: A growing middle class is increasingly mobile — and changing African ideas about leisure, travel and even the continent itself.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Carmen Abd Ali for The New York Times

item.title: Park Service Awards No-Bid Contract to Cover Bridge Statues in Gold
item.description: Originally estimated to cost $2.4 million, the government will now spend $5 million to restore the bronzes in time for Independence Day.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold

media:content.media:credit: Jared Soares for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Workers built scaffolding around one of the two The Arts of Peace statues near the Lincoln Memorial for a restoration project in Washington.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Won More Than 18,000 Votes Despite Suspending His Campaign
item.description: The former House member withdrew from the California governor’s race amid allegations of sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Eric Swalwell suspended his campaign for governor, but some Californians still voted for him.

item.title: Ebola Shows Why Humanitarian Aid Matters
item.description: The fecklessness of Washington leaders contrasts with the courage of doctors, nurses and aid workers in places like Congo.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kristof
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief
United States Agency for International Development

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
